summary: Applied hardened HTTP security headers on the shared nginx edge and verified the live header policy across every published hostname without changing the current platform version label.

verification.check: Merged edge playbook converged on nginx-lv3
verification.observed: The merged `playbooks/services/public-edge.yml` run completed successfully from the `Check whether the public edge certificate exists` task onward, reloaded nginx, and passed the built-in HTTP and HTTPS edge probes.
`uv run --with pyyaml python scripts/security_headers_audit.py` passed for api, build, changelog, docker, docs, grafana, nginx, ops, proxmox, sso, status, and uptime.

Direct `curl -sSI` probes for `docs.lv3.org` and `grafana.lv3.org` returned the expected HSTS, CORP, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, and X-Robots-Tag headers.

`nmap --script http-security-headers -p 443 docs.lv3.org grafana.lv3.org` reported the expected HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and CSP values on both representative public hosts after rollout.

notes: The full `make live-apply-service service=public-edge env=production` run from the integration worktree stalled while copying the already-generated docs and changelog site directories. The rollout then resumed from `Check whether the public edge certificate exists`, which converged the nginx configuration and verification path without changing the published static artifacts.
The edge now hides upstream security headers for proxied applications before adding the canonical repo-managed header policy.
